# Data — 4.21 MR Fingerprinting Reconstruction

## Committed sample (`sample/mrf_sample.txt`)

**This sample is SYNTHETIC.** It is generated by
[`../scripts/make_synthetic.py`](../scripts/make_synthetic.py) and contains **no
scanner data and no patient data**. It carries no clinical meaning and must never
be used for diagnosis (repo policy, CLAUDE.md §8).

### What it is

A tiny, self-contained MR Fingerprinting (MRF) reconstruction problem:

- **`T = 120` time frames** — the length of every fingerprint (the number of
  pseudorandom acquisition repetitions).
- **`D = 22` dictionary atoms** — candidate tissues, each a `(T1, T2)` relaxation-
  time pair whose simulated signal time course is one dictionary "fingerprint".
- **`V = 64` voxels** — the (synthetic) image pixels to reconstruct. Each voxel
  was drawn from a **known** dictionary atom, scaled by a random proton density
  (0.5–2.0), with a small amount of Gaussian noise (std = 0.5 % of the signal
  norm). The known source-atom index is stored as **ground truth** so the demo
  can report reconstruction accuracy.

### File format (whitespace-separated; `#` starts a comment)

```
T <int>                              # number of frames
D <int>                              # number of dictionary atoms
V <int>                              # number of voxels
ALPHA <T doubles>                    # flip angles per frame (radians)
TR    <T doubles>                    # repetition times per frame (ms)
TE    <T doubles>                    # echo times per frame (ms)
DICT                                 # then D lines, one per atom:
  <T1_ms> <T2_ms>                    #   the atom's relaxation times (T2 << T1)
SIGNAL                               # then V lines, one per voxel:
  <truth_atom> <T floats>            #   ground-truth atom index, then the signal
```

Parsed by `load_problem()` in [`../src/reference_cpu.cpp`](../src/reference_cpu.cpp).

### Provenance & how it is engineered

- **Generator:** `scripts/make_synthetic.py` (fixed RNG seed `421`, so the file
  is byte-for-byte reproducible: re-running the script regenerates it exactly).
- **Acquisition schedule:** a strong MRF-style flip-angle train (5–80°) with a
  periodic re-excitation envelope (to keep the T1-sensitive inversion-recovery
  transient alive) and an echo time `TE` that sweeps 15–120 ms (to give the T2
  dimension real leverage via `exp(-TE/T2)`).
- **Dictionary:** built from a candidate `T1 × T2` grid, then **greedily pruned**
  so no two kept atoms are within cosine `0.995` of each other. This yields a
  **well-separated** dictionary — the honest, reduced-scope teaching stand-in for
  a real 10⁵–10⁶-atom dictionary. Separation makes matching accurate **and**
  removes near-ties, so the GPU's SGEMM (which sums in a different floating-point
  order than the CPU) can never flip a voxel's `argmax`. See `../THEORY.md`.
- **Size:** ~104 KB, well under the repo's 50 MB commit limit — no Git LFS needed.

### Real MR Fingerprinting datasets (require registration; not redistributed)

The catalog lists these; obtain them yourself via
[`../scripts/download_data.ps1`](../scripts/download_data.ps1) /
[`.sh`](../scripts/download_data.sh), which print instructions and links and
**never** bypass any credential:

- **fastMRI** (includes quantitative/MRF-style data) — <https://fastmri.org/>
  (register + accept the data use agreement).
- **Cleveland Clinic MRF dataset** — via IEEE DataPort
  (<https://ieee-dataport.org/>, search "MR Fingerprinting"; verify the URL and
  access terms).
- **qMRI.org** quantitative-MRI resources — <https://qmri.org/> (verify URL).
- **Synthetic phantoms** — BrainWeb / XCAT digital phantoms for ground-truth maps.

### License

The synthetic sample and the generator are covered by the repository's MIT
license (code). No third-party data is committed, so no external data license
applies to `sample/`.
